page: Google Cloud Platform e2-standard-4 is a general-purpose virtual machine instance belonging to the e2 server family. It is configured with 4 vCPUs, which are mapped to 2 physical Intel Xeon cores utilizing hyperthreading with 2 threads per core. The instance features a dedicated CPU allocation running at a clock speed of 2.2 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. It is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced ratio of 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. This instance does not include bundled local storage or GPU accelerators, and it comes with zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark evaluations indicate that the e2-standard-4 delivers entry-level performance across most compute and memory workloads. In stress-ng testing, the instance achieved a single-core CPU score of 1105.89 and a multi-core score of 2907.35, placing it in the bottom 25% of tested hardware. Memory bandwidth tests show 161.16 GB/sec for cached small-block reads, dropping to 24.13 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads. Single-threaded gzip compression and Geekbench multi-core scores fall into the bottom 10% tier. However, the instance demonstrates average performance in text generation for small LLM inference models, reaching 104.71 tokens/sec, while static web serving throughput reaches 4.19 GB/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the e2-standard-4 offers a low-cost entry point for workloads that require dedicated CPU resources without demanding high-performance compute or massive memory bandwidth. The lack of local NVMe storage and GPUs limits its utility for intensive database operations or machine learning training. Instead, this instance is best suited for lightweight general-purpose workloads, such as basic web serving, small-scale Redis caching, minor background utility tasks, and development or testing environments where resource density is prioritized over raw execution speed.
description: Google Cloud Platform e2-standard-4 is a general-purpose virtual machine configured with 4 vCPUs and 16.0 GB of RAM. Powered by an Intel Xeon x86_64 processor running at 2.2 GHz, this instance features 2 physical cores with hyperthreading and a dedicated CPU allocation. It does not include local storage or GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks position this instance in the lower tier, with a single-core Geekbench score of 847.0 and a multi-core stress-ng score of 2907.35. However, it maintains average capabilities in text generation for small LLM models, achieving 104.71 tokens/sec. The e2-standard-4 is a cost-efficient option for lightweight workloads, making it suitable for basic web serving, development environments, and small-scale background tasks that do not require high-performance compute or local NVMe storage.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform e2-standard-4 is a general-purpose instance with 4 vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon CPU. It offers cost-efficient, entry-level performance for basic web serving and development.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform e2-standard-4 is an entry-level general-purpose instance with 4 vCPUs and 16 GB RAM, ideal for lightweight web serving.
tagline: A cost-efficient, general-purpose virtual machine with balanced resources for lightweight web serving, development, and basic background utility tasks.

Powered by a 2.2 GHz Intel Xeon x86_64 processor
Delivers entry-level performance suitable for basic workloads
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators included
